ops.title: cc-steer's local resident model stays lazy/light by design; the capability growth path is provider-agnostic scale-to-zero hosted serving, not a bigger local model
ops.body:
Decided 2026-07-18 during E2E planning: the local resident watcher model (Qwen3-8B-4bit MLX) was deliberately engineered to be a light, lazy resource citizen — do not grow it or propose bigger local models as the upside path. The growth path is **non-local serving on provider-agnostic scale-to-zero inference** (RunPod serverless, Modal, dedicated inference-only providers with custom-adapter hosting) — explicitly NOT Tinker-bound; Tinker sampling is one candidate provider among several. Any local MLX serving that does happen routes through rapid-mlx via athome, never a grown in-process mlx-lm path.

**Why:** the watcher's ~2-per-100-turn duty cycle makes traffic tiny and bursty — an always-on GPU (or a heavy resident model) is waste by construction; scale-to-zero matches the workload.

**How to apply:** when a sweep winner can't serve locally, default to evaluating hosted deployment (fused artifact, pinned revision, eval-what-you-serve through the actual endpoint) rather than a local serving workaround; local/hosted are backends of ONE serving abstraction in athome's serve layer ([[prefer-extend-declarative-over-parallel-subsystem]]). Weigh providers at duty-cycle economics, not headline $/Mtok.
